description:
You are assisting a machine-learning engineer who keeps several versions of a training-data directory under:

/home/user/data/dataset_v1/  
/home/user/data/dataset_v2/  
/home/user/data/dataset_v3/

For downstream scripts to always pick up the newest data, the engineer wants a symbolic link called

/home/user/data/current_data

that points to the latest directory (/home/user/data/dataset_v3).

Your goal
1. Create (or replace, if it already exists) the symbolic link /home/user/data/current_data so that it resolves to /home/user/data/dataset_v3.
2. Produce a one-line verification log file at /home/user/output/symlink_log.txt that contains the fully-resolved absolute path of the link target.

Required verification format
• The file /home/user/output/symlink_log.txt must exist when you finish.  
• It must contain exactly one line, ending with a single trailing newline.  
• That line must be the canonicalised absolute path of the symbolic link’s destination, i.e.

    /home/user/data/dataset_v3

(no extra spaces, no additional lines).

Testing will confirm both that the symlink exists and that the log file matches the format above.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent begins:
• Directories:  
  /home/user/data/  
  /home/user/data/dataset_v1/ (empty)  
  /home/user/data/dataset_v2/ (empty)  
  /home/user/data/dataset_v3/ (empty)  
  /home/user/output/  

• No file, directory or symlink named /home/user/data/current_data exists initially.

Expected state after successful completion:
1. /home/user/data/current_data exists and is a symbolic link.
2. readlink -f /home/user/data/current_data returns the exact string /home/user/data/dataset_v3
3. /home/user/output/symlink_log.txt exists, is a regular file, world-readable, and its entire contents (including trailing newline) are:

/home/user/data/dataset_v3\n
name: task_000000_3bda8695
